title: FloraDB Houseplant Care & Pet Toxicity (Sample)
subtitle: 100 houseplants with quantitative light/water metrics and ASPCA pet toxicity
description:
# FloraDB — Houseplant Care & Pet-Toxicity Dataset (Free Sample)

A structured dataset that turns subjective houseplant care advice — *"bright indirect light"*, *"water when dry"* — into **quantitative engineering metrics** (Lux thresholds, watering-day intervals, temperature and humidity ranges), joined to **ASPCA dog/cat toxicity** and grounded on the **GBIF Taxonomic Backbone**, with a source URL on every record.

This Kaggle dataset is the **free 100-plant sample** across 28 families. The full dataset — **270 curated care plants, 891 ASPCA toxicity records, a 20,000+ species GBIF taxonomic index, and 702 enriched species** — is available at [floradb](https://houseplants-botanical-floradb.pages.dev).

## What makes it useful

- **Quantitative care** — every plant has a light category mapped to a standard Lux band, plus watering-day interval, temperature range, and target humidity. Machine-ready for IoT planters and care apps.
- **ASPCA pet toxicity** — dog/cat determinations with clinical ingestion signs. `toxicity_status` is `aspca_verified`, conservatively `aspca_genus_inferred`, or `unknown` — **never guessed "safe"**.
- **GBIF-verified taxonomy** — accepted canonical names + `gbif_source_url` to re-verify each record; trade synonyms preserved.
- **Honest, graded quality** — `care_confidence` and `image_commercial_safe` flags let you filter to exactly the rows you trust.

## Field coverage (full care core, 270 plants)

| Field | Coverage | | Field | Coverage |
| :--- | ---: | --- | :--- | ---: |
| Scientific / common / family | 100% | | Native range | 96% |
| Light + Lux range | 100% | | Vernacular names | 81% |
| Watering / temp / humidity | 100% | | Toxicity determined | 63% |
| Representative image ref | 99% | | — species-verified (ASPCA) | 125 |

## Columns

See the column-descriptions tab — every field is documented. A starter notebook demonstrating loading, tabular preview, pet-safety filtering, and care-metric analysis is attached.

## Use cases

- Plant-ID and plant-care apps (structured care + pet-safety data)
- IoT smart planters (Lux targets and watering intervals as machine values)
- "Is this plant safe for my cat?" features backed by ASPCA determinations
- ML / RAG corpora over houseplant care and botany

## Sources & collection methodology

**Sources**
- **GBIF — Global Biodiversity Information Facility** (gbif.org) — Taxonomic Backbone for name verification, accepted names, family, vernacular names, and native range (GBIF Backbone is CC BY 4.0).
- **ASPCA Animal Poison Control** — toxic/non-toxic plant list; source of the dog/cat/horse toxicity flags and clinical signs (facts credited to ASPCA).
- **Image references** via GBIF occurrence media (e.g. iNaturalist) — one image URL per species under its own license (mostly CC BY-NC).
- **Curated horticultural references** — the normalized care metrics.

**Collection methodology**
1. Each name is matched live against the GBIF backbone; synonyms resolve to the accepted name and a re-verifiable `gbif_source_url` is stored on every row.
2. ASPCA toxicity was ingested honoring the site's robots.txt 10-second crawl-delay; `toxicity_status` records whether each determination is species-verified, conservatively genus-inferred, or unknown — never guessed "safe".
3. Care metrics are category-normalized (light category to a standard Lux band; watering/temperature/humidity from family profiles), each graded by `care_confidence`.

Updated quarterly (snapshot 2026.07).

## License & attribution

The **sample** is shared under **CC-BY-NC-4.0** (attribution, non-commercial). The **full commercial dataset** is licensed separately via [floradb](https://houseplants-botanical-floradb.pages.dev). Toxicity data is informational, not veterinary advice. Want a plant record corrected? Email floradb.hardhat456@simplelogin.com.

**Full dataset & updates:** https://houseplants-botanical-floradb.pages.dev

Prefer live, self-serve lookups instead of a snapshot? Run our actor on Apify: https://apify.com/dataengineered/houseplant-care-toxicity-lookup

resources.description: 100 curated houseplants across 33 families. One row per plant, with quantitative care metrics (Lux, watering days, temperature, humidity), ASPCA pet toxicity, GBIF-verified taxonomy, and enrichment (vernacular names, native range, image reference).

fields.name: plant_id
fields.description: Stable identifier for the plant record.

fields.name: synonym_scientific_name
fields.description: Widely-used horticultural/trade synonym, where it differs from the accepted name.

fields.name: light_requirement_level
fields.description: Standardized light category (Low to Direct Sun).

fields.name: min_lux
fields.description: Minimum light for active growth, in Lux (normalized from the light category).

fields.name: max_lux
fields.description: Maximum tolerated light before leaf scorch, in Lux.

fields.name: is_toxic_to_dogs
fields.description: 1 toxic, 0 safe, blank when toxicity_status is unknown.

fields.name: toxicity_status
fields.description: aspca_verified, aspca_genus_inferred, or unknown — never guessed 'safe'.

fields.name: vernacular_names_en
fields.description: Representative English common name from GBIF vernacular data.

fields.name: image_url
fields.description: Reference to one representative CC-licensed image (not redistributed).

fields.name: gbif_source_url
fields.description: GBIF species page the taxonomy was verified against (re-verifiable provenance).
